name: Today if ye will hear his voice
notes: The writer of Hebrews takes up Psalm 95:7-11, the Holy Ghost's call not to repeat the wilderness provocation, and presses it upon his readers across two chapters. Hebrews 3:7-11 sets out the full quotation almost word for word, and then the key phrases are sounded again: the opening 'To day if ye will hear his voice' returns at Hebrews 3:15 and 4:7, and the closing oath 'they shall not enter into my rest' returns at Hebrews 4:3 and 4:5. The shared core is the same warning from the same Psalm, with Hebrews drawing out that the rest God sware about is still open 'to day' to those who believe. Psalm 95 speaks the warning as Israel's worship; Hebrews keeps that vantage while applying it to the gospel hearer, noting the word was spoken 'in David' so long after the wilderness. Small wordings differ between the Psalm and its citation (heart and heart's, this generation and that generation, work and works); these are left as a matter for further study.
